title: G4: Degree bound of F independent of n (the binding constraint)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (uniform degree).** The coordinate functions of $\mathbf{F}$ must have degrees bounded by an absolute constant, independent of $n$; only the number $N$ of coordinates may grow with $n$.

The prior docs invoke Hochster–Huneke ('toric ideal generated in degree $\le$ dimension'), but the dimension here grows with $n$, so this yields an **$n$-dependent** bound and does NOT establish property (2). One needs a genuine local-to-global rigidity: a generating family in which each relation involves only a constant number of tuples (hence constant degree). This is the property that makes the problem interesting and is the main thing a correct proof must deliver (corresponds to P4).
